schedules.description: Trust/institution name, PAN, address, registration number (12A/12AB number), date of registration, objects of trust, sub-section under which filing (139(4A), 4B, 4C, 4D), whether income > ₹1Cr (triggering Form 10B audit).

keyFields.notes: Without valid registration number, exemption u/s 11 cannot be claimed.
139(4A) for charitable trusts; 139(4B) for political parties; 139(4C) for institutions; 139(4D) for universities/10(23C) entities.
Mandatory if income > ₹1 Crore. Form 10B filed separately by CA.

commonMistakes: Filing under wrong sub-section (e.g., 4A instead of 4D for universities)

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule AI — Details of Accumulation/Setting Apart u/s 11(2)
schedules.compulsoryCondition: If trust is accumulating more than 15% of income for future application
schedules.description: Under Section 11(1)(a), 15% of income can be retained without immediate application (set apart). If trust needs to accumulate MORE than 15%, it must file Form 9A (accumulation for specific purpose, max 5 years) or Form 10 (accumulation for projects). Schedule AI shows amounts and purposes.

keyFields.notes: Maximum 15% of income can be set apart without special approval.
Additional accumulation with approval. Purpose and timeline must be stated.

commonMistakes: Not investing accumulated amount in specified modes u/s 11(5)

schedules.scheduleName: Schedule ER — Application of Income (Expenditure on Religious/Charitable Purposes)
schedules.compulsoryCondition: Always — core schedule for Section 11 compliance
schedules.description: Detailed break-up of income received and how it was applied for charitable/religious purposes during the year. Total income received vs total applied. If applied ≥ 85%, fully exempt.

keyFields.notes: All receipts (excluding corpus donations) — donations, grants, rental income, investment income.

keyFields.fieldName: Total income applied for charitable purposes
keyFields.notes: Direct program expenses + admin expenses (reasonable portion). At least 85% of ER-1 must be applied.
Up to 15% of income that is retained (not applied but set apart for future).
EXCLUDE from income — corpus is a capital receipt, not income. Track separately.

commonMistakes: Including corpus donations in income (should not count toward 85% requirement)
Not counting admin expenses as application (reasonable admin costs count as application)
Using calendar year instead of financial year for application calculation

schedules.compulsoryCondition: Always — financial statement of the trust
schedules.description: Complete income and expenditure account of the trust showing all receipts (donations, grants, rental, interest, business income if any) and all payments (program expenses, admin, salaries, capital expenditure).

keyFields.notes: Regular donations forming part of income.

keyFields.fieldName: Income from property held under trust
keyFields.notes: Rent from properties owned by trust.

commonMistakes: Not maintaining separate accounts for corpus and non-corpus receipts

schedules.compulsoryCondition: If trust owns property earning rental income
schedules.description: Rental income from properties held by trust for charitable purposes. Normal HP computation applies.
commonMistakes: Not including rental income in 85% application calculation

schedules.description: For most compliant trusts: tax = 0 (85% application met). If not: 30% tax on income not applied/accumulated. Anonymous donations > 5% of income taxed at 30% u/s 115BBC. Accretion tax u/s 115TD for cancelled registrations.
commonMistakes: Not computing 115BBC tax on anonymous donations > 5% of total income
